name: Paracetamol 500mg
uses: Relief of mild to moderate pain (headache, muscle ache, backache, toothache)
Reduction of fever associated with common cold, flu, and infections
Management of joint pain and arthritis discomfort
sideEffects: Nausea or stomach upset (uncommon)
Allergic skin rash or itching (rare)
Liver toxicity (only with chronic overuse or severe overdose)
precautions: Do not exceed the maximum daily dose (usually 4g or eight 500mg tablets in 24 hours).
Avoid concurrent use with other paracetamol-containing medications (cough & cold remedies).
Consult a doctor if you have severe kidney or liver impairment or drink alcohol heavily daily.
storage: Store below 30°C (86°F) in a dry place. Keep protected from direct sunlight, dampness, and keep out of reach of children.

name: Cetirizine 10mg
uses: Treatment of seasonal allergic rhinitis (sneezing, runny nose, itchy or watery eyes)
Relief of hives (urticaria) and associated skin itching
Management of allergic dermatoses and insect bite reactions
sideEffects: Mild drowsiness, somnolence, or sleepiness
Dry mouth, throat irritation, or mild headache
Fatigue, dizziness, or stomach pain in sensitive individuals
precautions: May cause drowsiness; use caution when driving, operating heavy machinery, or performing critical tasks.
Avoid consuming alcohol or other central nervous system depressants while taking this medication.
Safety in children below 2 years and pregnant/breastfeeding individuals must be verified by a medical doctor.
storage: Store in a cool, dry place below 25°C (77°F). Protect from high moisture, heat, and direct light.

name: Amoxicillin 500mg
uses: Treatment of respiratory tract infections (bronchitis, pneumonia, tonsillitis, sinusitis)
Relief of middle ear infections (otitis media) and dental abscesses
Eradication of urinary tract infections (UTIs) of bacterial origin
sideEffects: Mild diarrhea or loose stools
Nausea, vomiting, or stomach discomfort
Oral thrush or vaginal yeast infection due to gut flora imbalance
Hypersensitivity skin rash (allergic response)
precautions: Strictly contraindicated in patients with a known history of severe penicillin or cephalosporin allergy.
Complete the entire prescribed bacterial course even if symptoms disappear to prevent antibiotic resistance.
Take with or immediately after food to reduce stomach upset.
storage: Store dry, below 25°C (77°F). If reconstituted as liquid suspension, keep refrigerated and discard unused portions after 14 days.

name: Azithromycin 500mg
uses: Eradication of atypical pneumonia, bronchitis, and throat infections (strep throat)
Treatment of sexually transmitted bacterial infections (chlamydia)
Management of certain skin, soft tissue, and sinus infections
sideEffects: Stomach cramps, abdominal pain, flatulence, or mild diarrhea
Nausea, loss of appetite, or transient hearing alterations (rarely with high doses)
Mild headache or dizziness
precautions: Do not take simultaneously with antacids containing aluminum or magnesium; separate them by at least 2 hours.
Inform your doctor if you have a history of heart rhythm issues (QT prolongation) or liver damage.
Ensure you complete the exact 3-day or 5-day cycle as instructed by your healthcare professional.
storage: Store below 30°C (86°F) in a moisture-protected container. Keep away from excessive humidity.

name: Pantoprazole 40mg
uses: Treatment of Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD, acid reflux, chronic heartburn)
Healing and prevention of stomach and duodenal ulcers
Management of pathological hypersecretory conditions (Zollinger-Ellison syndrome)
Prevention of stomach irritation from long-term NSAID pain reliever therapy
sideEffects: Mild headache, dizziness, or lightheadedness
Nausea, bloating, flatulence, or loose stools
Nutrient absorption changes (vitamin B12, magnesium) with prolonged multi-year usage
precautions: Best taken 30 to 60 minutes before breakfast (empty stomach) for optimal acid-blocking action.
Long-term daily use (over a year) may increase the risk of bone fractures; consult a doctor regarding calcium supplements.
Notify your doctor if you experience unexplained weight loss, recurring vomiting, or difficulty swallowing.
storage: Store in dry blister sheets below 25°C (77°F). Protect comprehensively from moisture and humidity.

name: Metformin 500mg
uses: Primary treatment for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus to lower blood glucose levels
Off-label management of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) to improve insulin sensitivity
Weight management assistance when combined with dedicated diet and exercise regimens under doctor supervision
sideEffects: Initial gastrointestinal distress (metallic taste in the mouth, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea)
Decreased absorption of Vitamin B12 with long term daily therapy
Lactic Acidosis (extremely rare but severe; marked by deep muscle breathing, extreme fatigue, cold limbs)
precautions: Must be taken with or immediately after meals to dramatically minimize stomach upset during initial stages.
Monitor kidney function (eGFR) and liver function tests regularly as prescribed by your doctor.
Strictly avoid excessive alcohol consumption white taking Metformin due to elevated lactic acidosis risk.
Avoid during severe dehydration, surgery, or major radiological imaging with contrast dyes.
storage: Store inside tightly closed original containers in dry environments below 30°C (86°F).

name: Omeprazole 20mg
uses: Fast relief of acid indigestion, heartburn, and sour stomach
Healing of esophageal erosion caused by acid reflux
Active eradication therapy for H. pylori bacterial infections when combined with antibiotics
sideEffects: Headache
Mild abdominal pain, nausea, loose stools or occasional constipation
Slight skin itch or dizziness
precautions: Take first thing in the morning with a full glass of water, minimum 30 minutes before any food.
Do not crush, break, or chew the delayed-release capsules; swallow them whole.
Long-term use may mask symptoms of other gastric conditions; seek medical review if indigestion persists beyond 2 weeks.
storage: Store tightly capped inside a light-resistant bottle below 25°C (77°F). Protect from excessive moisture.

name: Ibuprofen 400mg
uses: Reduction of painful swelling, joint stiffness, and active inflammation (rheumatoid arthritis, osteoarthritis)
Effective relief of severe dental pain, painful menstrual cramps, and physical injury pain
Quick relief of muscle aches, back pain, and fever
sideEffects: Heartburn, indigestion, abdominal pain, or nausea
Increased blood pressure or minor water retention (mild swelling of ankles)
Stomach ulceration or gastrointestinal bleeding (especially with long-term, high-dose unbuffered use)
precautions: Always take this medication with food or milk to safeguard the stomach lining against direct irritation.
Consult your physician if you have active gastric ulcers, high blood pressure, asthma, kidney, or heart issues.
Avoid concurrent usage of other NSAIDs (e.g. Aspirin, Naproxen) or blood thinners (e.g. Warfarin).
storage: Store away from intense heat, damp air, and direct sunlight. Keep below 25°C (77°F).
